question.title: BST: Analyze Given a Reference find previous
question.body:
I was looking at the code for given a reference to a node on a BST find the predecessor.

public static TreeNode findPredecessor(TreeNode node) {
    if (node == null)
        return null;

    if (node.getLeft() != null)
        return findMaximum(node.getLeft());

    TreeNode parent = node.getParent();

    TreeNode y = parent;
    TreeNode x = node;
    while (y != null && x == y.getLeft())
    {
        x = y;
        y = y.getParent();
    }


    return y;
}

I was just wondering what this part of the code does.

TreeNode y = parent;
TreeNode x = node;
while (y != null && x == y.getLeft())
{
    x = y;
    y = y.getParent();
}

return y;

what exactly is happening in the middle of the while loop?

Thank you!

answers.body:
In finding the predecessor there are two cases.

  1. If the node has a non-null left subtree, its predecessor would be the largest node in the left subtree.
  2. If it does not have a left subtree, then find the first node that is a right child if its parent (traversing upwards starting from the node of interest). Then, the parent node is the predecessor.
TreeNode y = parent; //parent is node.getParent(); i.,e parent of node.
TreeNode x = node; //assigns node to x
while (y != null && x == y.getLeft()) 
{
    x = y; //the parent becomes the current node 
    y = y.getParent(); //the parent is the parent of the parent
}

return y;

The while condition (y != null && x == y.getLeft()) says to continue till

  • parent is non-null and

  • the current node is a left child of its parent. i.e, keep going till a node that is a right child of its parent is found.

  • Return the parent node as the result.

Let's see this with an example

    40
 30    50
          60
        55
      54
    53

The in-order traversal gives 

30 40 50 53 54 55 60

The predecessor of 55 - the largest node in its left subtree - 54

The predecessor of 53 - the node, when traversing upwards, that is a right child of its parent is 60 - its parent 50 is the result.
